title: Auto import of GitHub repos to SonarQube Cloud in action.
markdown:
# Streamlining Code Quality: Automatic GitHub Repository Provisioning in SonarQube Cloud

## Introduction to Automatic Provisioning

SonarQube Cloud has introduced a new automatic provisioning feature that fundamentally simplifies the integration of GitHub repositories with code quality analysis. This innovative functionality eliminates the traditional manual setup process, allowing development teams to focus on coding rather than configuration. By creating a seamless connection between GitHub and SonarQube Cloud, the feature enables instant project provisioning without requiring any additional intervention from users.

## How the Feature Works

When a new repository is created in GitHub, the automatic provisioning feature triggers an immediate provisioning process in SonarQube Cloud. The newly bound project is automatically added to the corresponding SonarQube organization, establishing a direct connection between the GitHub repository and the cloud-based analysis platform. This synchronized workflow ensures that every new repository is immediately ready for code quality analysis without any manual configuration steps.

## Immediate Code Quality Insights

Once a project is provisioned, an initial scan begins automatically, delivering actionable insights from the very first commit. This immediate analysis capability allows development teams to gain visibility into code quality, security vulnerabilities, and other important metrics right from the project's inception. Rather than waiting for manual setup completion, teams can begin receiving quality feedback instantly, enabling them to maintain high standards from the beginning of development.

## Key Takeaways

- Automatic provisioning eliminates manual setup requirements when creating new GitHub repositories
- Newly created repositories are instantly provisioned and added to SonarQube Cloud organizations
- Initial scans begin automatically, providing code quality insights from the first commit
- The feature creates a seamless integration between GitHub and SonarQube Cloud platforms
- Development teams can focus on coding while infrastructure setup happens automatically
